entities.name: CATTLE CORRALLING
entities.description: The practice of penning livestock in specific plots to accumulate manure for soil fertility.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in dryland systems evaluated for yield response to cattle corralling.

entities.name: SAHELIAN FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: The agricultural systems located in the Sahel region of Africa.

entities.name: DRYLAND SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural environments characterized by low rainfall and water scarcity.

entities.name: SOIL NUTRIENT DYNAMICS
entities.description: The processes governing the availability and change of nutrients in the soil.

entities.name: SORGHUM YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of sorghum grain measured in kg per hectare.

entities.name: MANURE ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The buildup of organic waste deposited by livestock during corralling.

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: The organic component of soil, increased by manure deposition.

entities.name: TOTAL NITROGEN
entities.description: The total amount of nitrogen present in the soil.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: A primary soil nutrient that increases with longer corralling durations.

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: A primary soil nutrient that increases with longer corralling durations.

entities.name: SOIL PH
entities.description: The measure of soil acidity or alkalinity.

entities.name: MINERAL FERTILIZER
entities.description: Synthetic nutrient inputs used to enhance crop growth.

entities.name: NUTRIENT CYCLING
entities.description: The movement and exchange of organic and inorganic matter back into the production of living matter.

entities.name: AGROECOLOGICAL INTENSIFICATION
entities.description: The process of increasing agricultural productivity while maintaining ecological sustainability.

entities.name: CATTLE
entities.description: Livestock used in the corralling practice to provide organic inputs.

relationships.description: Increases grain yield significantly compared to unpenned plots
Influences the availability and levels of soil nutrients
Directly leads to the deposition and accumulation of dry manure
Increases the percentage of organic matter in the soil
Increases the total nitrogen content of the soil
Leads to greater accumulation of phosphorus in the soil
Leads to greater accumulation of potassium in the soil
Improves soil pH from strongly acidic to weakly acidic
Improvements in nutrient dynamics translate to higher grain yields
Reduces the requirement and use of mineral fertilizers
Enhances the natural cycling of nutrients in the farming system
Supports the goal of agroecological intensification in dryland systems
Cattle are the animals used to implement the corralling method
Sahelian farming systems are a type of dryland system
